basic_info.title: Castlevania
basic_info.review: Castlevania is a groundbreaking action-platformer that established the metroidvania genre with its atmospheric gothic horror setting, challenging gameplay, and memorable soundtrack by Michiru Yamane.

alternative_names: Akumajō Dracula (悪魔城ドラキュラ - Japanese)

play_today.details: The most accessible modern version with all classic Castlevania games included. Features save states, rewind functionality, and online leaderboards.

recommended_patches.name: Anniversary Collection Update
recommended_patches.description: Official update adding online leaderboards and improved save functionality

play_today.details: NES emulation provides the most authentic experience. Best played with FCEUX or Nestopia emulator for accuracy.

play_today.details: Play the original cartridge version via emulation. Best for purists wanting authentic experience.

description.synopsis: Simon Belmont, a member of the noble Belmont clan, must venture into Dracula's castle to stop his resurrection and save humanity from eternal darkness.
description.long_description: Castlevania, released in September 1986 for the Famicom Disk System in Japan (and May 1987 for NES in North America), is a landmark action-platformer developed by Konami. It established many conventions of the metroidvania genre and became one of the most influential games of the 8-bit era. Players control Simon Belmont, a vampire hunter from the noble Belmont clan, as he traverses Dracula's castle to prevent his resurrection. The game features challenging platforming, strategic combat with various weapons (whip, axe, dagger, holy water), and atmospheric gothic horror aesthetics that defined the series for decades. While the MSX version titled 'Vampire Killer' exists with different gameplay mechanics, the NES/Famicom version is universally considered the definitive original. The game's iconic soundtrack by Michiru Yamane remains one of gaming's most beloved compositions.
legacy_and_impact: Created one of gaming's most recognizable franchises
Michiru Yamane's soundtrack became legendary in gaming history
Set standards for atmospheric horror in video games
Continues to influence modern indie developers decades later
